zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Coleman於1620年8月16日出生在英國素福爾可布倫特 艾萊時，他的父親Sir Richard Coleman30歲，母親Lady Rebecca Claiborne27歲。他於1650年與Elizabeth Grizzell在美國維吉尼亞州艾可結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1652年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。他於1637年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加被列為occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff。他去世於1689年8月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州格盧斯特享年69歲，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Coleman于1620年8月16日出生在英国素福爾可布倫特 艾萊时，他的父亲Sir Richard Coleman30岁，母亲Lady Rebecca Claiborne27岁。他于1650年与Elizabeth Grizzell在美国弗吉尼亚州艾可结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1652年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州。他于1637年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加被列为occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff。他去世于1689年8月在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州格盧斯特享年69岁，埋葬在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加維吉尼亞州韓諾福。

nameMeanings.name: Coleman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Kalman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Bela, Gabor, Sandor, Imre, Lajos, Geza, Gyula, Katalin, Zoltan.

Hungarian (Kálmán): from the old Hungarian personal name Kálmán, meaning ‘remainder’ (from Turkic kal ‘to remain’), hence a protective name, which was given to infants to ward off evil and harmful spirits. In medieval times the name became particularly popular because it was confused with the German Christian name Koloman of Irish origin (see Kolman 2).

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the Yiddish personal name Kalmen, an everyday form of Kloynemes (from Hebrew Kalonimos, which is from Greek kalos ‘lovely’ or kallos ‘beauty’ + onyma ‘name’). This Hebrew name is first recorded in the Talmud and has been used continuously since then. Among Hungarian Jews, it was sometimes confused with 1 above.

variants.name: Colman
meanings: Irish (Mayo and Sligo) and English: variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kohlmann   or   Kollmann  .
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

variants.name: Clifford
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Collman
meanings: English (Middlesex and Surrey): variant of   Coleman  .
Americanized form of German   Kollmann  , or of   Kohlmann  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Coleman was born on 16 August 1620, in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, his father, Sir Richard Coleman, was 30 and his mother, Lady Rebecca Claiborne, was 27. He married Elizabeth Grizzell in 1650, in Essex, Virginia, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. He immigrated to Virginia, British Colonial America in 1652. In 1637, at the age of 17, his occupation is listed as occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff in British Colonial America. He died in August 1689, in Gloucester, Virginia, British Colonial America, at the age of 69, and was buried in Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Coleman est né le 16 août 1620, à Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Coleman, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Lady Rebecca Claiborne, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Grizzell en 1650, à Essex, Virginie, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Il a immigré au Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1652. En 1637, à l’âge de 17 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff à Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé en août 1689, à Gloucester, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterré à Hanover, Virginie, Amérique coloniale britannique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Coleman am 16. August 1620 in Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Coleman 30 und seine Mutter Lady Rebecca Claiborne 27. Er hat 1650 in Essex, Virginia, Vereinigte Staaten Elizabeth Grizzell geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er ist 1652 nach Virginia, British Colonial America eingewandert. 1637, als er 17 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff in British Colonial America aufgeführt. Er starb im August 1689 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Gloucester, Virginia, British Colonial America und wurde in Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Coleman è nato il il 16 agosto 1620 nel Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard Coleman, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Lady Rebecca Claiborne, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Grizzell nel 1650, nel Essex, Virginia, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Immigrato nel Virginia, British Colonial America nel 1652. Nel 1637, all'età di 17 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff nel British Colonial America. Morì nel agosto 1689, nel Gloucester, Virginia, British Colonial America, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolto a Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Colemanが1620年8月16日にイギリススフォルクブレント エレイグで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Colemanは30歳，母Lady Rebecca Claiborneは27歳であった。1650年にアメリカ合衆国バージニア州エセックスでElizabeth Grizzellと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1652年にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州に移住した。1637年，17歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカでの職業はoccupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriffと記録されている。彼は1689年8月にイギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州グロウセスターで69歳で死去し，イギリス植民地アメリカバージニア州ハノバーにて埋葬された。

ko.Castle Sowerby with Sebergham, Cumberlan: 영국 c벌랜드 카스틀 소워비 위스 세버그함
ko.lifeSummary: Robert Coleman(이)가 1620년 8월 16일 영국 수폴크 브렌트 엘리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Coleman(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Rebecca Claiborne(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1650년 미국 버지니아 주 에세스에서 Elizabeth Grizzell(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1652년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주로 이민을 떠났다. 1637년, 17세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아에서 occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff이었다. 그는 1689년 8월 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 그루스스터에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 버지니아 주 핸버에 묻혔다.

pt.Castle Sowerby with Sebergham, Cumberlan: Castle Sowerby with Sebergham, Cumberland, Inglaterra
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Coleman nasceu em 16 de agosto de 1620, em Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Coleman, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Lady Rebecca Claiborne, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Grizzell em 1650, em Essex, Virgínia, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Virginia, British Colonial America em 1652. Em 1637, com 17 anos, sua profissão aparece como occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff em British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em agosto de 1689, em Gloucester, Virginia, British Colonial America, com 69 anos, e foi sepultado em Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America.

ru.Abingdon Parish, Gloucester, Virginia, B: абингдон пэриш, глоусестир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Charles City County, Virginia, British C: чарльс сити коунти, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.Castle Sowerby with Sebergham, Cumberlan: кастл соуирби вит себиргам, кумбирланд, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Coleman родился 16 августа 1620 г. в местности брент элиг, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Coleman было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Rebecca Claiborne, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Grizzell в 1650 г. в местности эссекс, Вирджиния, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он иммигрировал в Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1652 году. В 1637 году, в возрасте 17 лет,  он работал по профессии occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff в местности бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер август 1689 г. в местности глоусестир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 69 лет и был похоронен в местности хановир, Виргиния, бритиш колоняль амирика.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Coleman nació el 16 de agosto de 1620, en Brent Eleigh, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Coleman, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Lady Rebecca Claiborne, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Grizzell en 1650, en Essex, Virginia, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Inmigró a Virginia, British Colonial America en 1652. En 1637, a la edad de 17 años, su ocupación registrada era occupation: sailed to america in 1637, headright, sheriff en British Colonial America. Murió en agosto de 1689, en Gloucester, Virginia, British Colonial America, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultado en Hanover, Virginia, British Colonial America.
